Ah! The user is saying they're on a different WiFi network. That makes sense - if they're on a different network, their phone number might be routing differently or they might not be reachable at the same number, or there could be other network-related issues.

However, looking at the logs, the actual problem is that Asterisk is trying to connect to the bot via `localhost` which resolves to IPv6 `::1` first, and the bot is only listening on IPv4 `0.0.0.0`. This is a technical issue, not related to the WiFi network.

But the user is suggesting the WiFi change might be the cause. Let me think about this:

1. The call IS being placed to the Twilio trunk successfully (we see the channel being created)
2. The phone is ringing (based on the pattern we're seeing)
3. The problem is that when answered, Asterisk can't connect to the bot

This is definitely the IPv6/IPv4 issue I identified, not the WiFi network. The WiFi network wouldn't affect the internal connection between Asterisk and the bot.

I should acknowledge their thought but explain that the actual issue is the localhost resolution problem, and I need to fix the dialplan. Let me fix it quickly.

content.text: The WiFi wouldn't affect the bot connection - the issue is that Asterisk is trying to connect to the bot via IPv6 first (`::1`) but the bot only listens on IPv4. I need to fix the dialplan to use `127.0.0.1` instead of `localhost`. Let me do that quickly:

;================================
; Ava AudioSocket Configuration
;================================

[ava-context]
; Bot instance extensions - connect to Ava via AudioSocket
exten => 9092,1,NoOp(Connecting to Bot 1 via AudioSocket)
 same => n,Answer()
 same => n,Set(JITTERBUFFER(adaptive)=25,1000,20)  ; LOW LATENCY: 25ms target
 same => n,Wait(2)  ; TCPA: 2-second delay before connection
 same => n,Set(UUID=${SHELL(uuidgen | tr -d '\n')})
 same => n,Set(CONTACT_NAME=TestCaller)

 same => n,Hangup()

;================================
; Ava AudioSocket Configuration
;================================

[ava-context]
; Bot instance extensions - connect to Ava via AudioSocket
exten => 9092,1,NoOp(Connecting to Bot 1 via AudioSocket)
 same => n,Answer()
 same => n,Set(JITTERBUFFER(adaptive)=25,1000,20)  ; LOW LATENCY: 25ms target
 same => n,Wait(2)  ; TCPA: 2-second delay before connection
 same => n,Set(UUID=${SHELL(uuidgen | tr -d '\n')})
 same => n,Set(CONTACT_NAME=TestCaller)
 same => n,NoOp(Dialing AudioSocket - UUID: ${UUID})
 same => n,AudioSocket(${UUID},172.17.0.1:9092)
 same => n,Hangup()

exten => 9093,1,NoOp(Connecting to Bot 2 via AudioSocket)
 same => n,Answer()
 same => n,Set(TIMEOUT(absolute)=300)  ; Keep call alive 5 minutes minimum
 same => n,Set(JITTERBUFFER(adaptive)=25,1000,20)  ; LOW LATENCY: 25ms target
 same => n,Wait(2)  ; TCPA: 2-second delay before connection
 same => n,Set(UUID=${SHELL(uuidgen | tr -d '\n')})

exten => 9094,1,NoOp(Connecting to Bot 3 via AudioSocket)

 same => n,NoOp(Dialing AudioSocket - UUID: ${UUID})
 same => n,AudioSocket(${UUID},172.17.0.1:9094)
 same => n,Hangup()

; Echo test extension for testing SIP connection
exten => 600,1,NoOp(Echo Test)
 same => n,Answer()
 same => n,Playback(demo-echotest)
 same => n,Echo()
 same => n,Playback(demo-echodone)
 same => n,Hangup()

; Status extension
exten => 500,1,NoOp(System Status)
 same => n,Answer()

 same => n,SayNumber(${EPOCH})

; Predictive dialer - wait for answer then connect to bot
exten => wait,1,NoOp(Predictive dialer - waiting for answer)

 same => n,Set(UUID=${SHELL(uuidgen | tr -d '\n')})
 same => n,Set(BOT_PORT=${CHANNEL(endpoint)})
 same => n,NoOp(Call answered - UUID: ${UUID})
 same => n,Hangup()

; ========================================
; Call Monitoring (ChanSpy)
; ========================================

; Monitor all active calls (cycles through channels)
exten => 700,1,NoOp(Call Monitoring - Spy on all channels)
 same => n,Answer()
 same => n,Wait(1)
 same => n,ChanSpy(PJSIP,qB)  ; q=quiet, B=barge mode disabled, cycles through channels
 same => n,Hangup()

; Monitor specific bot port (701 = bot 1, 702 = bot 2, 703 = bot 3)
exten => 701,1,NoOp(Monitor Bot 1 on port 9092)
 same => n,Answer()
 same => n,Wait(1)
 same => n,ChanSpy(PJSIP,q)  ; Quiet mode - listen only
 same => n,Hangup()

exten => 702,1,NoOp(Monitor Bot 2 on port 9093)
 same => n,Answer()
 same => n,Wait(1)
 same => n,ChanSpy(PJSIP,q)
 same => n,Hangup()

exten => 703,1,NoOp(Monitor Bot 3 on port 9094)
 same => n,Answer()
 same => n,Wait(1)
 same => n,ChanSpy(PJSIP,q)
 same => n,Hangup()

; Whisper mode - can talk to bot (not recommended for AI bots)
exten => 710,1,NoOp(Monitor with whisper mode)
 same => n,Answer()
 same => n,Wait(1)
 same => n,ChanSpy(PJSIP,qw)  ; w=whisper mode
 same => n,Hangup()
; Asterisk Dialplan Configuration for Exodus Dialer

; Purpose:
; - Handle outbound calls originated by dialer_orchestrator.py
; - Bridge answered calls to AudioSocket bot instances
; - Support predictive dialing with call parking

;================================
; Ava AudioSocket Configuration
;================================

[ava-context]
; Bot instance extensions - connect to Ava via AudioSocket
exten => 9092,1,NoOp(Connecting to Bot 1 via AudioSocket)
 same => n,Answer()
 same => n,Set(JITTERBUFFER(adaptive)=25,1000,20)  ; LOW LATENCY: 25ms target
 same => n,Wait(2)  ; TCPA: 2-second delay before connection
 same => n,Set(UUID=${SHELL(uuidgen | tr -d '\n')})
 same => n,Set(CONTACT_NAME=TestCaller)
 same => n,NoOp(Dialing AudioSocket - UUID: ${UUID})
 same => n,AudioSocket(${UUID},172.17.0.1:9092)
 same => n,Hangup()

exten => 9093,1,NoOp(Connecting to Bot 2 via AudioSocket)
 same => n,Answer()
 same => n,Set(TIMEOUT(absolute)=300)  ; Keep call alive 5 minutes minimum
 same => n,Set(JITTERBUFFER(adaptive)=25,1000,20)  ; LOW LATENCY: 25ms target
 same => n,Wait(2)  ; TCPA: 2-second delay before connection
 same => n,Set(UUID=${SHELL(uuidgen | tr -d '\n')})
 same => n,Set(CONTACT_NAME=TestCaller)
 same => n,NoOp(Dialing AudioSocket - UUID: ${UUID})

exten => 9094,1,NoOp(Connecting to Bot 3 via AudioSocket)
 same => n,Answer()

 same => n,Wait(2)  ; TCPA: 2-second delay before connection
 same => n,Set(UUID=${SHELL(uuidgen | tr -d '\n')})
 same => n,Set(CONTACT_NAME=TestCaller)
 same => n,NoOp(Dialing AudioSocket - UUID: ${UUID})
 same => n,AudioSocket(${UUID},172.17.0.1:9094)
 same => n,Hangup()

; Echo test extension for testing SIP connection
exten => 600,1,NoOp(Echo Test)
 same => n,Answer()
 same => n,Playback(demo-echotest)
 same => n,Echo()
 same => n,Playback(demo-echodone)
 same => n,Hangup()

; Status extension
exten => 500,1,NoOp(System Status)
 same => n,Answer()
 same => n,Playback(hello-world)
 same => n,SayNumber(${EPOCH})
 same => n,Hangup()

; Predictive dialer - wait for answer then connect to bot
exten => wait,1,NoOp(Predictive dialer - waiting for answer)
 same => n,Answer()
 same => n,Wait(2)  ; TCPA: 2-second delay before connection
 same => n,Set(UUID=${SHELL(uuidgen | tr -d '\n')})
 same => n,Set(BOT_PORT=${CHANNEL(endpoint)})
 same => n,NoOp(Call answered - UUID: ${UUID})
 same => n,Hangup()

; ========================================
; Call Monitoring (ChanSpy)
; ========================================

; Monitor all active calls (cycles through channels)
exten => 700,1,NoOp(Call Monitoring - Spy on all channels)
 same => n,Answer()
 same => n,Wait(1)
 same => n,ChanSpy(PJSIP,qB)  ; q=quiet, B=barge mode disabled, cycles through channels
 same => n,Hangup()

; Monitor specific bot port (701 = bot 1, 702 = bot 2, 703 = bot 3)
exten => 701,1,NoOp(Monitor Bot 1 on port 9092)
 same => n,Answer()
 same => n,Wait(1)
 same => n,ChanSpy(PJSIP,q)  ; Quiet mode - listen only
 same => n,Hangup()

exten => 702,1,NoOp(Monitor Bot 2 on port 9093)
 same => n,Answer()
 same => n,Wait(1)
 same => n,ChanSpy(PJSIP,q)
 same => n,Hangup()

exten => 703,1,NoOp(Monitor Bot 3 on port 9094)
 same => n,Answer()

 same => n,ChanSpy(PJSIP,q)
 same => n,Hangup()

; Whisper mode - can talk to bot (not recommended for AI bots)
exten => 710,1,NoOp(Monitor with whisper mode)

; Asterisk Dialplan Configuration for Exodus Dialer
